title: phlegmariurus colanensis
intro: Phlegmariurus colanensis, a species of the Phlegmariurus genus, is a type of epiphytic plant also known as a climbing fern, belonging to the lycopodiopsida class, which includes club mosses and quillworts. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits associated with Phlegmariurus colanensis include the potential treatment of various conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its application in wound healing and skin care. Herbal preparations of Phlegmariurus colanensis often involve the plant's leaves and stems being dried and then used as infusions or decoctions in traditional medicine.
